The race is expected to be extremely tight in Paris with Emmanuel Grégoire, heading a united left and green list, competing with conservative Rachida Dati and hard-left La France

news.headline: Moment Iranian strike hits central Tel Aviv
news.summary: Watch the moment an Iranian strike hits what appears to be a bridge in central Tel Aviv, startling pedestrians nearby.

news.headline: Hormuz crisis underlines vulnerability of global trade chokepoints
news.summary:

news.headline: 2 women climb out of window to flee Hong Kong building fire that injured 3 others
news.summary:
Two women climbed out of a window of a residential building in Hong Kong to escape a fire that sent three people to hospital on Sunday, police said.
Police received multiple reports at 5.28pm of a blaze at Yen Dack Building on Chun Yeung Street in North Point.
Thick smoke billowed from a high-floor

news.headline: US hub in Iraq targeted overnight by Iranian drones, rockets
news.summary:
Eight overnight attacks targeted a US diplomatic and logistics centre at Baghdad’s International Airport, an Iraqi security official said on Sunday.
Several waves of departures from the US facility occurred on Saturday from the airport, according to another Iraqi security source, speaking on conditi

news.headline: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
news.summary: The strike killed 64 people, including 13 children, two nurses and a doctor, according to the WHO head.

news.headline: 1,200 musicians set Guinness World Record at concert in Hong Kong
news.summary:
More than 1,200 musicians set a Guinness World Record for the largest reed ensemble featuring the sheng – a traditional Chinese free-reed instrument – at a Hong Kong concert on Sunday.
The “Endless Sheng, Thousand Reeds in Harmony” marathon opened the International Sheng and Reeds Festival of the Ho
